ID = "STR_HISTORY_TECH_FLINT_WEAPONS"   ;   Str = "Armory weapon upgrades affect all human soldiers, heroes and ships. They do not affect myth units or siege weapons. All attacks are affected, including hand-to-hand hack attacks, ranged pierce attacks, and even ranged hack attacks, such as that of the Throwing Axeman.\n\nFlint was one of the most important materials used by early humans because it could be shaped into extremely sharp edges. It did not need to be melted; instead, it was shaped by striking it in a process called knapping. When broken correctly, flint produced razor-sharp flakes that were made into tools such as arrowheads, spear points, and knives. Its sharpness and reliability made it essential for hunting and everyday survival."
ID = "STR_HISTORY_TECH_JADE_WEAPONS"   ;   Str = "Armory weapon upgrades affect all human soldiers, heroes and ships. They do not affect myth units or siege weapons. All attacks are affected, including hand-to-hand hack attacks, ranged pierce attacks, and even ranged hack attacks, such as that of the Throwing Axeman.\n\nJade was a highly valued stone in early civilizations because of its beauty and strength. It could not be melted, but it was carefully ground and polished into tools, ornaments, and ceremonial objects. Although difficult to shape, jade was tougher than many other stones and less likely to break, making it both practical and symbolic."
ID = "STR_HISTORY_TECH_OBSIDIAN_WEAPONS"   ;   Str = "Armory weapon upgrades affect all human soldiers, heroes and ships. They do not affect myth units or siege weapons. All attacks are affected, including hand-to-hand hack attacks, ranged pierce attacks, and even ranged hack attacks, such as that of the Throwing Axeman.\n\nObsidian was a valuable material for early civilizations because it could produce some of the sharpest edges of any natural stone. Formed from cooled volcanic lava, it did not need to be melted but could be shaped by striking it in a process called knapping. When broken, obsidian formed razor-sharp blades that were used to make knives, spear points, and arrowheads. Obsidian tools were sharper than most stone tools, though they were more fragile, making them powerful but delicate weapons for hunting and warfare."
ID = "STR_HISTORY_TECH_FEATHERED_ARMOR"   ;   Str = "Armory armor upgrades affect the hack armor of all human soldiers, heroes, and ships. They do not affect myth units or siege weapons. Hack armor protects a unit against hack attacks, which are generally hand-to-hand attacks such as swords and axes. The armor percentage is the amount by which the attack is reduced.\n\nCarefully gathered, arranged, and bound onto clothing, armor, and headdresses, feathers are valued for their light weight and brilliant colors. Feathered armor is worn more for display, ritual, and status than for true defense. Its beauty and rarity means it is reserved for elite warriors and nobility."
ID = "STR_HISTORY_TECH_CEREMONIAL_ARMOR"   ;   Str = "Armory armor upgrades affect the hack armor of all human soldiers, heroes, and ships. They do not affect myth units or siege weapons. Hack armor protects a unit against hack attacks, which are generally hand-to-hand attacks such as swords and axes. The armor percentage is the amount by which the attack is reduced.\n\nCeremonial Armor is created for display, ritual, and tradition. It is often crafted with great care, using decorative materials, bright colors, and symbolic designs to represent power and status. Ceremonial Armor is worn by leaders, champions, or honored warriors during important events, serving as a symbol of authority and prestige."
ID = "STR_HISTORY_TECH_SACRED_ARMOR"   ;   Str = "Armory armor upgrades affect the hack armor of all human soldiers, heroes, and ships. They do not affect myth units or siege weapons. Hack armor protects a unit against hack attacks, which are generally hand-to-hand attacks such as swords and axes. The armor percentage is the amount by which the attack is reduced.\n\nSacred Armor is forged not only for protection, but for spiritual purpose. It is crafted with rare materials and marked with holy symbols and enchantments to guard the wearer from both physical and unseen dangers. Though fully functional in battle, its true power comes from divine favor rather than its strength alone. Sacred Armor is worn by chosen champions, temple guardians, and holy warriors."
ID = "STR_HISTORY_TECH_FEATHERED_SHIELDS"   ;   Str = "Armory shield upgrades affect the pierce armor of all human soldiers, heroes and ships. They do not affect myth units or siege weapons. Pierce armor protects a unit against pierce damage, which is generally inflicted by ranged attacks such as arrows. The armor percentage is the amount by which the attack is reduced.\n\nFeathers are prized for their light weight and striking appearance. Feathers are carefully layered and bound onto wooden or woven frames to create protective surfaces that are easier to carry than heavier materials. While not as strong as metal, feathered shields offer some cushioning against blows, whilst add ceremonial and aesthetic value."

They are often made from ornate materials, bright colors, and intricate designs to symbolize authority, honor, or spiritual power. Ceremonial shields inspire, and convey status during processions and on the battlefield."

Pierce armor protects a unit against pierce damage, which is generally inflicted by ranged attacks such as arrows. The armor percentage is the amount by which the attack is reduced.\n\nSacred Shields are forged to protect not just the body, but the spirit. Crafted with rare materials and inscribed with holy symbols and enchantments, they repel both physical attacks and mystical threats. ID = "STR_HISTORY_TECH_AMBUSH"   ;   Str = "Ambush Tactics trains warriors to strike from a distance before the enemy can react. By refining positioning, timing, and surprise maneuvers, infantry catch enemies off guard and turning the tide of battle. Masters of this art can control the battlefield, hitting hard while remaining elusive and unpredictable."
ID = "STR_HISTORY_TECH_CHINAMPAS"   ;   Str = "Chinampas are floating gardens that transform ordinary farms into highly productive plots. By weaving soil and vegetation over shallow waters, they allow villagers to gather food more efficiently, sustaining larger communities and fueling the growth of cities. These fertile channels turn humble fields into lifelines of abundance, ensuring prosperity even in the heart of waterways."
ID = "STR_HISTORY_TECH_TZOMPANTLI_TOWER"   ;   Str = "Tzompantlis or skull racks have been documented in several Mesoamerican civilizations. They consisted of a scaffold-like construction of poles, mounted with human heads or skulls. These structures were used for public display of war captives or other sacrificial victims.\n\nTzompantli Towers differ from Sentry Towers, by collecting the life force (tonalli) of warriors who fall within range. Once upgraded to Tzompantli Watch Towers, they can shoot enemies, and slow their movement.\n\nRanged attacks have difficulty hitting fast-moving targets, such as cavalry. Research Crenellations to improve the chance to hit such units.\n\nTzompantli Towers typically cannot attack units directly beneath them, but Tlaloc’s technology Burnt Water will permit them to do so."
ID = "STR_HISTORY_TECH_TZOMPANTLI_WATCH_TOWER"   ;   Str = "The Tzompantli Watch Tower collects tonalli from warriors who have fallen in the surrounding lands. It channels the fear of the dead into its arrow attacks, with a chance of slowing enemies on hit.\n\nMore than a weapon, it stands as a sacred sentinel; A symbol of divine power that inspires allies and strikes fear into the hearts of any who dare approach."
ID = "STR_HISTORY_TECH_TEMIMINALOYAN_TRIALS"   ;   Str = "Temiminaloyan is the 6th level of Mictlan, the Aztec underworld, where the dead are pierced with arrows, shot by the invisible hands of warriors who were lost in battles.\n\nTemiminaloyan Trials channels the souls of these fallen warriors, allowing your Tzompantlis to fire a third arrow on each attack, with a greater ability to slow enemies."
ID = "STR_HISTORY_TECH_WARRIORS_HONOR"   ;   Str = "Warrior’s Honor respects the bravery and sacrifice of every warrior who faces the enemy. When a soldier falls, a fragment of their tonalli lingers, a spark of spirit that can strengthen the living. The courage of your own warriors is readily felt and woven into the strength of your people, while the essence of fallen foes must be claimed through ritual by a Warrior Priest, transforming the echoes of battle into both devotion and power."
ID = "STR_HISTORY_TECH_SACRIFICE_SETTLER"   ;   Str = "The offering of one’s life is the highest act of devotion to the Aztec gods, a ritual of ultimate surrender and faith. This act demonstrates unwavering loyalty and courage, drawing the attention of the divine. Those who are first to take this bold action are especially honored, their communities blessed with prosperity, protection, and the gods’ guiding presence."
ID = "STR_HISTORY_TECH_SACRIFICE_LIVESTOCK"   ;   Str = "Livestock sacrifice honors the gods through the offering of animals, a ritual of gratitude and devotion. The sacrifice of livestock is believed to bless the land and its people, bringing abundance and vitality to the fields, forests, and waters. Through this act, the spirits watch over the community, ensuring prosperity and the steady growth of life."
